bottom_line: CR-00074 delivered a clean, well-executed cross-project isolation test matrix (14 tests, 4 axes) with zero genuine leaks found, zero fix cycles, zero operator recovery needed, complete TDD RED evidence on both axes, and all 8 quality gates passing on first attempt. KNOWN_LEAK is empty (0 Incidents filed). The matrix is now part of the regression suite.
coverage_notes: All logs read in full. S01_run4.log (2.1KB) is the canonical S01 log; S01_run1/run2 were empty (execution failures from earlier runs). S09 log is the largest at ~416KB; used grep+tail for error patterns. S08 (unit-tests) and S11 (security-secrets) logs small. Reports dir contains all 11 step reports (S01-S11). DB telemetry: DB:UP verified via step lifecycle. No archived tarball needed.

findings.title: No genuine cross-project isolation leaks found on main — establish periodic regression cadence
findings.recommendation: The isolation matrix found 0 leaks on main, confirming the project's current isolation posture. Establish a periodic run cadence (e.g., weekly CI trigger) for `make test-isolation` so future regressions are caught before the quarterly full integration suite. The matrix is fast (~15s) and self-contained.
evidence: ai-dev/work/CR-00074/reports/CR-00074_S01_Backend_report.md — 'KNOWN_LEAK allowlist (keyed by route/command) → empty; the _xfail_marks helper attaches xfail(strict=True) automatically for any future entry. No genuine isolation leak found on main.'

ai-dev/work/CR-00074/reports/CR-00074_S01_Backend_report.md — 'No genuine cross-project leak was found on main: every project-scoped route handler and iw command audited filters correctly by project_id. 0 high-priority Incidents filed.'

findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add periodic isolation-matrix regression run to CI cadence; analyzed in CR-00074 (see ai-dev/work/CR-00074/reports/CR-00074_self_assess_report.md). Target: Makefile or CI config. Effort: M.

findings.title: dual_project_seed.py had 3 latent import-level bugs in earlier S01 run
findings.recommendation: The earlier S01 run (CR-00074_S01_run1.log, S01_run2.log — both empty) left a dual_project_seed.py with three import-killing bugs: NameError from forward-referencing ProjectIds in default_factory, WorkItem(item_type=...) using invalid kwarg (should be type= with WorkItemType enum), and duplicate-PK IntegrityError from re-creating test-proj. The subsequent run fixed all three. Consider adding a `python -c "import tests.fixtures.dual_project_seed"` smoke test to the Makefile target so fixture import failures surface immediately.
evidence: ai-dev/work/CR-00074/reports/CR-00074_S01_Backend_report.md — 'The file left by an earlier run did not import at all (three latent bugs: ProjectIds referenced in field(default_factory=...) before its own definition → NameError at import; WorkItem(item_type="feature", ...) — item_type is not a WorkItem column, the real column is type and takes a WorkItemType enum; and seed_two_projects re-created the test-proj Project already inserted by test_project → duplicate-PK IntegrityError). The rewrite exposes seed_two_projects(session, proj_a=None)'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add fixture import smoke test to Makefile test-isolation target; analyzed in CR-00074 (see ai-dev/work/CR-00074/reports/CR-00074_self_assess_report.md). Target: Makefile. Effort: S.

findings.title: iw next-id is a global allocator excluded from Axis 2 — follow-up could add positive assertion
findings.recommendation: The matrix correctly excluded iw next-id from Axis 2 (id_sequences is global, not project-scoped). A follow-up CR could add a positive assertion that next-id is indeed global (returns the same sequence regardless of project scope), converting the exclusion into a confirmed property. This is LOW priority.
evidence: ai-dev/work/CR-00074/reports/CR-00074_S01_Backend_report.md — 'iw next-id is not project-scoped — id_sequences is a global per-prefix allocator. Correctly excluded from Axis 2 (documented in the module).'
